HKJK | Jomo Kenyatta | Nairobi, Kenya

>> Go to this page.

HKJK About

Jomo Kenyatta International Airport is an international airport in Nairobi, the capital of and largest city in Kenya. The other three important international airports in Kenya include the Kisumu International Airport, Moi International Airport and Eldoret International Airport. JKIA is located in the Embakasi suburb 18 kilometres (11 miles) southeast of Nairobi's central business district, the airport has scheduled flights to destinations in over 50 countries. Originally named Embakasi Airport, the airport's name was changed in 1978 to honour Jomo Kenyatta, Kenya's first president and prime minister.

Aerodrome Data

ICAO HKJK
IATA NBO
Elevation 5330ft
Transition Altitude 9000 ft
Transition Level FL100
Mag Variation 0.56º E
Coordinate 01°19′07″S 36°55′33″E
Site http://kaa.go.ke/

Position Callsign Frequency Remarks

HKJK_GND Nairobi Apron 121.600 -

Airspace Class Owner Limits Remarks

HKJK Delivery

  • HKJK doesn't have a dedicated Delivery Position, with Nairobi Apron (HKJK_GND) assuming the role of Delivery.

IFR Clearance Rules

VFR Clearance Rules

VFR aircraft clearance must come from the tower controller, but the ground controller should get some information from the pilot and taxi them out to a runway before they receive their clearance.

When a VFR aircraft calls for clearance, they must include the following: - Souls on board - Fuel on board - Intentions

If the aircraft does not include any of the above, the information must be requested before a clearance is given.

Once all the information has been received from the pilot, the controller must then issue start clearance and taxi instructions to a runway holding point. Once the aircraft is at the holding point, they can receive their clearance.

At this point the aircraft must be handed to tower so that the tower controller can provide the aircraft with a clearance.

Cruise Level Rules

* Kenyan Airspace operates with the following RVSM standard table of Cruising Levels.

Magnetic Track 000º to 179º 180º to 359º
Flight Rules IFR VFR IFR VFR
Flight Level 010 - 020 -
030 035 040 045
050 055 060 065
070 075 080 085
090 095 100 105
110 115 120 125
130 135 140 145
150 155 160 165
170 175 180 185
190 195 200 205
210 215 220 225
230 235 240 245
250 255 260 265
270 275 280 285
290 - 300 -
310 - 320 -
330 - 340 -
350 - 360 -
370 - 380 -
390 - 400 -
410 - 430 -
450 - 470 -
490 - 510 -
530 - 550 -
570 - 590 -
etc - etc -

HKJK Ground

Push-back Procedures

Phraseology

CALLSIGN, push-back approved, facing DIRECTION.
Apron Type Facing Remarks
Apron 1 Main Terminal / Remote North or North-West or South or South-East If Medium-Haul, taxiways K and M may be used.
Apron 2 Cargo North-East -
Apron 3 General Aviation South-East Only start-up will be needed in most cases.

Apron 1 Stand Allocation

  • Pilots may request to choose a different stand at their discretion.
  • If preferred stand is occupied, then redirect aircraft to a stand in the same terminal or the J parking stands.
Terminal Stands
1A 15-21
1B 14-9
1C 8-4
1D 2A-C & 3A-C
2 22A-F
Terminal ICAO Airline Preferred Stand Remarks
1A ABY Air Arabia 19 -
AFR Air France 18 -
CSN China Southern ANY -
KQA Kenya Airways ANY International Only / Can also use Terminals 1B to 1C.
KLM KLM Royal Dutch Airlines ANY -
SVA Saudia 18 -
N/A Other SkyTeam Airlines ANY -
1B AIC Air India 11 -
ATC Air Tanzania ANY -
BAW British Airways 14 -
ETH Ethopian Airlines 12 -
QTR Qatar Airways 10 -
SAA South African Airways ANY -
SWR Swiss International Air Lines ANY -
UGD Uganda Airlines 11 -
N/A Other Oneworld Airlines ANY -
1C MSR EgyptAir 7 -
UAE Emirates 7 -
ETD Etihad Airways ANY -
DLH Lufthansa 5 -
MWI Malawian Airlines 4 -
RWD RwandAir ANY -
THY Turkish Airlines 8 -
N/A Other Star Alliance Airlines ANY -
1D JMA Jambojet ANY -

2 AXK African Express Airways ANY -

JBW Jubba Airways ANY -
PRF Precision Air ANY -

HKJK Tower

Runway Selection

  • Runway 06 is the preferred active runway, up to 10 kts of tailwind.

Runway Change Operations

  • Runway changes must be coordinate with existing DEL, GND, TWR, DEP and APP controllers.
  • If possible, hold aircraft on stand before a runway change operation.
  • If an aircraft is already taxying on Twy G, offer the pilot the option to use the existing active Rwy or to enter the runway for taxiway direction change.
  • If an aircraft as already passed an approach IAF fix, an APP or TWR controller should advise the pilot of the possibility of a runway change prior to the aircraft being on the final approach segment.

Wake Separation

Arrivals (nm)

Lead J H M L
J
H 6 4
M 7 5 5
L 8 6 5 5

Departures (min)

Lead J H M L
J
H 2
M 3 2
L 3 2 2

Missed Approach Procedures

HIRO

  • When HIRO Ops are in effect, Tower Controllers will only handover traffic to Ground Controllers after issuing initial taxi instructions to pilots to avoid runway exit congestion.
  • If possible, Tower Controllers should advise pilots on which exit they should expect to vacate.
Rwy Exit Taxi Instructions Remarks
06 B 
Taxi Right on ALPHA
 GA Aircraft Only
C 
Taxi Left on ALPHA
D (High Speed) 
Hold Short of RWY06 until advised to cross onto L
 -
E 
Taxi Right on GOLF
 -
F -
L -
24 A 
Taxi Right on ALPHA
 GA Aircraft Only
B
C 
Taxi Left on ALPHA
E 
Taxi Right on GOLF
 -
F -
K 
Taxi Left/Right on GOLF
 -

>> Go to this page.
>> Go to this page.

HKJK Departure

  • HKJK doesn't have a dedicated Departure Position, with Nairobi Radar (HKJK_APP) assuming the role of Departure.

Departure Procedures

  • All aircraft are required to fly the assigned SID given by ATC. Controllers may issue continuous climb instructions to FL145.

Coordinating HKNW & HKRE Departures

  • As HKNW and HKJK are under 10nm apart, and HKRE and HKJK are roughly 6nm apart, when operating as Nairobi Radar (HKJK_APP), the controller should have full awareness of the surrounding area, and must be careful to keep any outbound HKNW flights away from inbound HKJK traffic, which could cause a potential conflict.
  • If the HKNW traffic is departing to the south, it may be necessary to delay the flight in order to prioritise incoming traffic bound for HKJK.
  • The APP controller should always coordinate with Wilson Tower or Ground (if online) in regards to coordination for flights departing, in order to prevent major conflicts.

HKJK Jomo Kenyatta Intl | v0.1

>> Go to this page.
>> Go to this page.

Approach Procedures

Rwy Approach Frequency Course GP Angle IAF Platform Remarks
06 ILS 110.30 NL 053º 3.00º 5.4 DME NL 7100 ft -
ILS W 110.30 NL 053º 3.00º 11.9 DME NL 9100ft -
ILS Y 110.30 NL 053º 3.00º 9.4 DME NL 8300ft -
ILS Z 110.30 NL 053º 3.00º 6.9 DME NL 7500ft -

Holds

Name ILS Approach Altitude Speed (IAS) Inbound CRS Turn Remarks
NV ILS / RNP FL100 230 KT 106º Right RWY06
ILS W 129º
STONY - 9000ft 230 KT 287º Left -

Missed Approach Procedures

Rwy Approach Missed Approach Procedure Hold Remarks
06 ILS Climb on Runway Heading to 6000ft or NV VOR, then turn right climbing to 9000ft to the TV VOR and hold, or as directed. STONY -
ILS W -
ILS Y -
ILS Z -
RNP Proceed direct APDUS climbing to FL100. Passing 8100ft or APDUS, turn right direct to TV VOR and hold, or as directed. -
VOR DME Climb on Runway Heading to 6000ft or NV VOR, then turn right climbing to 9000ft to the TV VOR and hold, or as directed. -
24 RNP Proceed direct IBRAT climbing to FL100. Passing 9100ft or IBRAT, turn left direct to TV VOR and hold, or as directed. -
VOR DME Climb on R-233 NV to 6000ft or D3.4, then turn left climbing to 9000ft to TV VOR and hold, or as directed. -

MSA and MRVA

Radio Aid Min Distance Max Distance Radial From Radial To MSA Remarks
NV 0 nm 25 nm 090º 180º 9600 ft -
0 nm 25 nm 180º 270º 8100 ft -
0 nm 25 nm 270º 360º 8000 ft -
0 nm 25 nm 360º 090º 9100 ft -

Diversions

ICAO Name Distance Longest Rwy Remarks
HKNW Wilson Airport 8 nm 1560m -
HKMO Moi International Airport 262nm 3350m -
HKKI Kisumu International Airport 174nm 3300m -

HTKJ Kilimanjaro International Airport 145nm 3600m -

HKJK Coordination

HKJK & HKNW

  • HKJK Controllers, particularly Tower and Approach, should be mindful of simultaneous operations at HKJK and HKNW. In most circumstances, most aircraft taking off from HKNW will be taking off to the south, which can cause a conflict for inbound traffic landing on 06. In the event of HKNW traffic, releases from HKJK should be coordinated with Approach, if online, however in most circumstances, HKJK traffic will be given priority.

HKJK Jomo Kenyatta Intl | v0.1

>> Go to this page.

HKJK Charts

Unfortunately, Kenya does not provide publicly accessible navigational charts for its airspace or airports.

So, the most we can do at this point is to offer you a link to Google's Search Results.

>> Go to this page.

HKJK Pilot Briefing

Pilots taking part in events that depart from or arrive to HKJK should make themselves familiar with this briefing page as well as with all existing charts.

Overview

  • HKJK Nairobi Jomo-Kenyatta International Airport is Nairobi's most busiest international airport, capable of supporting a higher volume of traffic movements and aircraft types. It is a single runway (06/24) airport with RNAV and ILS (06 only) approaches and multiple airport parking positions.

Departure Briefing

Clearance Delivery

  • HKJK does not have a real world Delivery position or in VATSIM. All delivery clearances will typically be handled by the GND controller, or whoever is online.
  • When requesting IFR clearance, please provide your Aircraft Type, Stand Number and ATIS letter.
  • When reading back the clearance, make sure to readback the transition with the SID (if provided).

Departure Procedures

  • Pilots should report to the controller once airborne before being handed off, or being given further instructions to climb or vectors.
  • HKJK has many varying SIDS with different transitions. Please double check before filing, and the initial flight climb will be provided when requesting IFR clearance for departure.

Arrival Briefing

Arrivals and Approaches

* As your flight is approaching HKJK, you should make yourself familiar with the existing Arrivals (STARs), Holding Points and Approaches.

* Be aware that HKJK has 4 distinct sets of ILS Approaches (ILS RWY 06, X, Y and Z) and that they have distinct approach procedures. Controllers will inform you what Approach to expect based on an assigned Arrival.

* HKJK is a single runway airport, so during Arrival Events, you should expect to Hold.

* In case of Missed Approach, climb 9000ft ft and follow the missed approach procedure for your specific approach. You'll receive further instructions by the Controllers.

* During busy events, when vacating the runway, it is recommended to taxi straight right onto G in order to prevent a backlog of traffic exiting the runway, however if traffic is taxiing in front, give way to the traffic unless advised by ATC.

Parking and Ground Operations

  • HKJK has plenty of parking positions across multiple aprons. Aircraft will be initially assigned to a specific terminal and stand according to their airline. However, during busier events, aircraft may be redirected to a “J” parking position, or another terminal in order to ease congestion. If there is no available parking on the south side of the airport, aircraft may be redirected to park in the General Aviation side of the airport, guaranteed that there is suitable room to fit any more aircraft.

HKJK Jomo Kenyatta Intl | v0.1
